section.title: Scope
section.para: XLIFF is the XML Localization Interchange File Format designed by a group of multilingual content publishers, software providers, localization service providers, localization tools providers, and researchers. It is intended to give any multilingual content owner a single interchange file format that can be understood by any localization provider, using any conformant localization tool. While the primary focus is on being a lossless interchange format, usage of XLIFF as a processing format is neither encouraged nor discouraged or prohibited.

This document is one part of a multi-part Work Product that defines Version &version; of XLIFF. XLIFF Version &version;. Part 1: Core presents the XLIFF core, which is the minimum set of XML elements and attributes required to create a valid XLIFF file. XLIFF Version &version;. Part 2: Extended presents the XLIFF core as well as the optional Modules that MAY be used along with the core for advanced functionality.

para.emphasis: All text is normative unless otherwise labeled.
